title: Playground Learning Library
concept: A saved-sites-first Playground redesign for first-time WordPress learners where saved and unsaved Playgrounds are the primary library objects, creation and portability are inline actions on that library, and the active WordPress site becomes a secondary preview once a Playground is selected.
focus: saved-sites library as the first viewport
modal-free inline panels for creation, saving, management, and portability
friendly beginner UI with restrained color blocks
active site preview kept secondary to library selection
flows: Select saved or unsaved Playgrounds from a card library, then open, save, rename, delete, or manage them inline.
Start a new Playground from Vanilla WordPress, WordPress PR, Gutenberg PR or branch, GitHub import, Blueprint URL, or .zip import without leaving the library workspace.
Save temporary work to browser storage or a local directory with visible copy progress.
Use Site Manager tabs for settings, file browser, blueprint editing, database tools, and logs while keeping the selected Playground context visible.
Browse and filter the Blueprint gallery with featured shortcuts and all 43 entries represented.
Move work through GitHub export, .zip download, Blueprint bundle actions, and database.sqlite download.
featureCoverage: import from GitHub after account connection
edit WordPress version, PHP version, and language
file browser with create file, create folder, upload, browse, and code editing affordances
view, copy, download, and run current Blueprint bundle
inspect database driver, path, and size
inspect Playground, WordPress, and PHP logs
